food_subgroup: Fried meat snack combo (meat balls and chicken wings)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a combined Thai meat ball and chicken wing snack box. FOODON_03315595 (chicken wing food product) is used as the closest parent anchor for the chicken wing component. The meat ball component has no closer FoodOn match.
ontology.foodex2_description: Poultry meat and poultry meat products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Poultry meat and poultry meat products) is the closest defensible parent for this mixed snack combo containing chicken wings and meat balls. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for this composite Thai snack product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this composite Thai snack combo product.

serving.description: 1 snack box (4 meat balls + 4 chicken wings/drumettes)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is one snack box containing approximately 4 pieces of beef or chicken ball and 4 pieces of chicken wing and drumette, estimated at approximately 280 g total.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken wing, cooked, fried (SR Legacy ID 05062) and Meat balls, beef, cooked (SR Legacy ID 22904), combined weighted estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB Singapore entry exists for this composite Thai snack box. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted combination of USDA SR Legacy data for fried chicken wings (approximately 50% by weight) and cooked beef/chicken meat balls (approximately 50% by weight). Thai seasoning contribution to macros is minor and within estimation uncertainty. Values represent a plausible per-100g composite estimate. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Analogous estimate based on fried meat and poultry products (GI approximately 25–35).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this Thai snack combo. GI is estimated at 30 based on analogous fried meat and poultry products from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. Fried meat products with minimal carbohydrate content consistently fall in the low GI range (GI 25–35). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (30 × 4.5 × 2.80) / 100 = approximately 3.8; however, using per-100g carbohydrate of 4.5g and a reference 100g portion: GL = (30 × 4.5) / 100 = 1.35, rounded to 1.3.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100g; estimated ~1456 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this snack box due to its high sodium content from seasoning, fish sauce, and processing; a single serving may contribute over 60% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index but high fat content and caloric density
type2_diabetes.guidance: While the low GI (estimated 30) and minimal carbohydrate content are favourable for blood glucose control, the high total fat and caloric density of this fried snack combo require portion control for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and sodium from deep-frying and seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This fried snack combo contains saturated fat (~4.8 g/100g), trans fat (~0.2 g/100g), cholesterol (~85 mg/100g), and high sodium, all of which are risk factors for cardiovascular disease; individuals with CVD should consume this product only occasionally and in small portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Thai Snack Combo Box (Meat Balls and Chicken Wings) is a popular Thai-inspired snack platter served at Thai casual dining restaurants in Singapore, such as Saap Saap Thai. One serving consists of four pieces of beef or chicken ball and four pieces of chicken wing and drumette, totalling approximately 280 g. The meat balls are seasoned with Thai spices including garlic, lemongrass, and fish sauce, then deep-fried or grilled to achieve a juicy interior and lightly crispy exterior. The chicken wings and drumettes are marinated in Thai-style seasonings and deep-fried until golden and crispy. The flavour profile is savoury, mildly spicy, and aromatic, characteristic of Thai street food. Nutritionally, this combo is protein-rich (~18.5 g protein per 100g) and energy-dense (~245 kcal per 100g), with moderate-to-high fat content (~16.5 g fat per 100g) due to deep-frying. Sodium content is high (~520 mg per 100g), making it a concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 30 (low), making it relatively blood-glucose-friendly, though the caloric density warrants portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. This dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding gluten or fish-derived ingredients. It is commonly consumed as a shared appetiser or snack in a group dining setting.

confidence_reason: No exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central for this specific composite Thai snack box product. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted combination of USDA SR Legacy data for fried chicken wings and cooked meat balls. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for fried meat products. Confidence is medium due to the composite and restaurant-specific nature of this product.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source data for these component foods. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source data for these component foods. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source data for these component foods. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported in the cited source. Serving size of 280 g is an estimate based on typical restaurant portion weights for 4 meat balls (~80 g) and 4 chicken wings/drumettes (~200 g). Halal status is listed as false/unknown as this cannot be confirmed from available public data for this specific restaurant and product. The cuisine_tags 'Pad Thai' and 'Thai Noodle' from the input record are not applicable to this snack combo and have been excluded. GI estimated at 30 based on analogous fried meat and poultry products from the University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category is low.
